headline: US and Iran trade strikes, threatening fragile truce
description: Iran's central military command accused the US of violating the ceasefire with attacks in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, saying they struck US forces in retaliation. The US says Iran attacked three American destroyers first, which Trump later downplayed and said he considered the ceasefire still active.

description: News of passenger infections on the MV Hondius cruise ship emerged in early May, bringing hantaviruses into the spotlight. Here are the main scientific terms you need to know.

headline: Ghanaian author Nana Darkoa Sekyiamah challenges stereotypes about African women's sexuality
description: In her book 'The Sex Lives of African Women,' Sekyiamah, a well-known figure in Ghanaian and African feminist circles, depicts the intimate lives of 31 women from the continent and the diaspora.

headline: After PSG qualifies for the Champions League final, fans face travel headaches getting to Budapest
description: Paris Saint-Germain supporters hoping to attend the match against Arsenal on May 30 in the Hungarian capital must face high prices and the club's very selective ticket allocation process.

headline: How sanctioned doctors kept practicing across the French-Belgian border
description: Because they fail to consult European alerts on suspensions or removals, France and Belgium allow doctors with problematic pasts to practice in their neighboring country.

headline: Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
description: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

headline: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.
